qtype: Basic number operations, place value, powers, roots, reciprocals, index laws, order of operations, rounding
tex:
\begin{question*}{1}{a}Write the number forty thousand and thirty-three in figures.
\newline

(b)Find the value of $\sqrt[3]{729}$.
\newline

(c)Find the reciprocal of $\frac{7}{9}$.
\newline
\\
Give your answer as a decimal, correct to 3 decimal places.
\newline

(d)Find the value of $6^5 \div 3^4$.
\newline

(e)Work out $(- 9) \times (- 7) \div (- 3)$.
\newline

(f)Work out.
\newline

(i) $11 + 9 \times 5 - 4$

(ii) $(11 + 9) \times 5 - 4$

(g)
\[
\frac{5}{9}  -0.67  \sqrt{123}  \sqrt{49}  3.142
\]
From this list, write down an irrational number.
\newline

(h)
\begin{subpartsaliged}
Find the lowest common multiple (LCM) of 24 and 104.
\newline

Find the highest common factor (HCF) of 24 and 104.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

\end{question*}

content.content:

\newline

Find the reciprocal of $\frac{7}{9}$.
\newline
\\

\newline

Find the value of $6^5 \div 3^4$.
\newline

(e)Work out $(- 9) \times (- 7) \div (- 3)$.
\newline

(f)Work out.
\newline

(i) $11 + 9 \times 5 - 4$

(ii) $(11 + 9) \times 5 - 4$

(g)
\[
\frac{5}{9}  -0.67  \sqrt{123}  \sqrt{49}  3.142
\]
From this list, write down an irrational number.
\newline

(h)
\begin{subpartsaliged}
Find the lowest common multiple (LCM) of 24 and 104.
\newline

Find the highest common factor (HCF) of 24 and 104.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

\end{question*}

qtype: Fraction of an amount, ratio division, percentage increase (compound/simple interest)
tex:
Antonio buys a restaurant for \$$240\,000$.
\newline
\\
This is $\frac{5}{8}$ of the amount he has available to spend.
\newline

(a) Show that he has \$$144\,000$ left after buying the restaurant.
\newline

(b) Some of the \$$144\,000$ is spent on expenses.
\newline
Expenses are wages, equipment and supplies in the ratio

\[
\text{wages} : \text{equipment} : \text{supplies} = 9 : 5 : 8.
\newline
\]

The amount spent on wages is \$$45\,000$.
\newline

(i) (c) Find the amount spent on

\begin{subsubparts}

(d) equipment

(e) supplies.
\newline

\end{subsubparts}

(f) Work out the amount Antonio has left now.
\newline

(g) Antonio borrows \$$25\,400$ for 6 years at a rate of 5\% per year simple interest.
\newline

Calculate the total amount he repays at the end of the 6 years.
\newline

(h) In one week, the number of customers in the restaurant was 560.
\newline
In the next week, the number of customers in the restaurant was 656.
\newline

Calculate the percentage increase.
\newline

content.content:
Show that he has \$$144\,000$ left after buying the restaurant.
\newline

Some of the \$$144\,000$ is spent on expenses.
\newline
Expenses are wages, equipment and supplies in the ratio

\[
\text{wages} : \text{equipment} : \text{supplies} = 9 : 5 : 8.
\newline
\]

The amount spent on wages is \$$45\,000$.
\newline
Find the amount spent on
equipment
supplies.

Work out the amount Antonio has left now.
\newline

Antonio borrows \$$25\,400$ for 6 years at a rate of 5\% per year simple interest.
\newline

Calculate the total amount he repays at the end of the 6 years.
\newline

In one week, the number of customers in the restaurant was 560.
\newline
In the next week, the number of customers in the restaurant was 656.
\newline

Calculate the percentage increase.
\newline

stem.content:
Antonio buys a restaurant for \$$240\,000$.
\newline
\\
This is $\frac{5}{8}$ of the amount he has available to spend.
\newline

qtype: plotting quadratic and linear graphs, line of symmetry
tex:
(a) Complete the table of values for $y = 5 + 3x - x^2$.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
$x$ & $-2$ & $-1$ & $0$ & $1$ & $2$ & $3$ & $4$ & $5$ \\
\hline
$y$ & & $1$ & & $7$ & & & & $-5$ \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(b) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = 5 + 3x - x^2$ for $-2 \leqslant x \leqslant 5$.
\newline

\begin{InsertScreenShot}

\end{InsertScreenShot}

(c) Write down the equation of the line of symmetry of the graph.
\newline

(d) (i) (e) Complete the table of values for $y = 2x + 1$.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
$x$ & $-1$ & $0$ & $2$ \\
\hline
$y$ & & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(f) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = 2x + 1$ for $-2 \leqslant x \leqslant 5$.
\newline

(g) Write down the coordinates of the two points where the two graphs intersect.
\newline

content.content:
Complete the table of values for $y = 5 + 3x - x^2$.
\newline

\newline

Write down the equation of the line of symmetry of the graph.
\newline

Complete the table of values for $y = 2x + 1$.
\newline

On the grid, draw the graph of $y = 2x + 1$ for $-2 \leqslant x \leqslant 5$.
\newline

Write down the coordinates of the two points where the two graphs intersect.
\newline

qtype: Speed calculation from travel graph, time duration calculation
tex:
Two friends, Diego and Javier, meet at a swimming pool.
\newline
The travel graph shows Diego's journey by bicycle from his home to the swimming pool.
\newline

\begin{InsertScreenShot}

\end{InsertScreenShot}

(a) Calculate Diego's speed for his journey from his home to the swimming pool.
\newline
Give your answer in kilometres per hour.
\newline

(b) Diego stays at the swimming pool until 12 20.
\newline

(i) (c) On the grid, draw the line representing the time he stays at the swimming pool.
\newline

(d) Work out how long, in hours and minutes, he is at the swimming pool.
\newline

(e) Javier leaves his home 15 minutes later than Diego.
\newline
He walks to the swimming pool at a constant speed of 6 km/h.
\newline

On the grid, show Javier's journey from his home to the swimming pool.
\newline

(f) They both leave the swimming pool at 12 20 and return to their own homes, each at a constant speed.
\newline
Diego arrives home at 12 45.
\newline
Javier arrives home 5 minutes later than Diego.
\newline

Complete the travel graph.
\newline

content.content:
Calculate Diego's speed for his journey from his home to the swimming pool.
\newline
Give your answer in kilometres per hour.
\newline

Diego stays at the swimming pool until 12 20.
\newline

On the grid, draw the line representing the time he stays at the swimming pool.
\newline

Work out how long, in hours and minutes, he is at the swimming pool.
\newline

Javier leaves his home 15 minutes later than Diego.
\newline
He walks to the swimming pool at a constant speed of 6 km/h.
\newline

On the grid, show Javier's journey from his home to the swimming pool.
\newline

They both leave the swimming pool at 12 20 and return to their own homes, each at a constant speed.
\newline
Diego arrives home at 12 45.
\newline
Javier arrives home 5 minutes later than Diego.
\newline

Complete the travel graph.
\newline

stem.content:
Two friends, Diego and Javier, meet at a swimming pool.
\newline
The travel graph shows Diego's journey by bicycle from his home to the swimming pool.
\newline
